steps.title: Extract the circle's center and radius
steps.content:
The equation $(x+4)^2 + (y-19)^2 = 121$ is in standard form.
Center: $(-4, 19)$
Radius: $\sqrt{121} = 11$

steps.title: Determine which x-coordinate is on the circle
steps.content:
For a point $(a, b)$ on the circle, $(a+4)^2 + (b-19)^2 = 121$.
Rearranging: $(b-19)^2 = 121 - (a+4)^2$.
For $b$ to be real, we need $(a+4)^2 \leq 121$.

Backsolving — testing choice B ($a = -14$):
$(a+4)^2 = (-14+4)^2 = (-10)^2 = 100 \leq 121$ ✓

Testing the others:
  • A) $(-16+4)^2 = (-12)^2 = 144 > 121$ — Fails
  • C) $(11+4)^2 = 15^2 = 225 > 121$ — Fails
  • D) $(19+4)^2 = 23^2 = 529 > 121$ — Fails
That matches choice B — B is correct.
